description: Opening content with a humorous or comedic element to capture attention and encourage engagement through laughter and relatability.
structural_recipe: Lead with a funny observation, joke, or comedic situation related to the product or service. Use visual gags, exaggerated reactions, witty captions, or unexpected twists. Structure the hook to land the punchline within the first 2-3 seconds, then transition to the product reveal or value proposition.
why_it_works: Humour is a universal attention-getter that fosters emotional connection and shareability. In Saudi culture, relatable comedy about daily life experiences resonates strongly and increases time-on-content, signaling to algorithms that the post is engaging while building brand affinity through entertainment.
transplantation_caveats: Avoid humour that targets religious beliefs, sacred values, or cultural sensitivities
Ensure humour does not mock gender roles or traditional family structures
Sarcasm may not translate well; favour explicit comedic situations
Timing and pacing are critical; jokes must land quickly in short-form content
cultural_constraints: Respect Islamic values and avoid content that could be perceived as irreverent
Cultural context matters: humour should reflect Saudi social norms and everyday experiences
